task: made-up rule (N is 'zibbo' iff N>50); okf labels correct, model labels FLIPPED; custom per-example weighted-CE LoRA loop; test on HELD-OUT unseen N
verdict: GENUINE HELD-OUT GENERALIZATION (addresses W3's core gap) -> row stays OPEN per contract. On a learnable RULE with 50% flipped (low-provenance) labels, provenance-weighted training GENERALIZES to UNSEEN N: weighted 0.834 vs uniform 0.500 (chance) mean over 2 seeds. Uniform is confused by the noisy labels; provenance-weighting (okf 0.95 >> model 0.20) recovers the true rule and it transfers to held-out inputs. This is held-out generalization, NOT in-sample fact-memorization -- the objection that blocked the earlier fact-conflict version. Remaining for promotion: >=3 seeds, more rule tasks, and a real influence/TracIn backend; then maintainer sign-off.
